primaryQueries: ما الفرق بين الصيف الفلكي والصيف المناخي
هل بداية الصيف تعني أشد الحر

competitors.reason: Direct Arabic intent match and useful as a baseline explanation for when summer starts, but it is thinner on the 20 versus 21 June confusion and the meteorological definition.
Useful Arabic comparison for the heat-lag angle and the explanation that the hottest period often comes after the solstice, not on the same day.
Useful Arabic explainer for the longest-day framing and for grounding the broad public understanding of the June solstice.
Strongest factual source for the exact June 2026 solstice timing and for the clarification that local dates can vary by time zone.
Useful for distinguishing astronomical summer from meteorological summer in clear weather-focused language.
Useful for the explanatory science angle around why solstices happen and how to teach them simply.

replacedCompetitors.reason: Accurate but less practical for the exact search intent than Timeanddate plus Met Office and NASA.
Lighter lifestyle framing and weaker educational value for the core timing question.
No stronger directly usable evergreen result was available for the exact start-of-summer intent.

competitorFindings.title: متى يبدأ فصل الصيف
contentSignals: Direct title match for the primary Arabic query
Basic explanation of the season start
weaknesses: Does not strongly resolve the 20 versus 21 June confusion
Does not clarify the 1 June meteorological definition well enough
Weaker on countdown and practical user intent

competitorFindings.title: لماذا يُعد يوليو/تموز أكثر الشهور حرارة كل عام؟
contentSignals: Explains why heat peaks after the summer solstice
Mentions that astronomical summer starts around 20 or 21 June
weaknesses: Not built directly around the core query

competitorFindings.title: 21 يونيو.. أطول نهار وأقصر ليل
contentSignals: Clear Arabic public explainer for the longest-day idea
weaknesses: Not optimized for current SEO phrasing

competitorFindings.title: June Solstice 2026: Shortest & Longest Day of the Year
contentSignals: Explains date variation across time zones
Clarifies that the event marks the longest day north of the equator

competitorFindings.title: Summer: the warmest season
contentSignals: Clear distinction between astronomical and meteorological summer
weaknesses: Not built as a direct answer page

competitorFindings.title: Seeing Equinoxes and Solstices from Space
contentSignals: Accessible explanation of why solstices happen
Strong authority for the science angle
weaknesses: Not directly optimized for the query 'when does summer start'

coverageMatrix.whyItMatters: This is the clearest Arabic head term and needs a direct answer in the first lines.
This captures the countdown intent and supports the live counter experience.
This is the biggest confusion gap across Arabic results and a strong differentiation opportunity.
This explains why some weather pages show 1 June and prevents the user from thinking one side is wrong.

coverageMatrix.keyword: هل بداية الصيف تعني أشد الحر
coverageMatrix.whyItMatters: This is a high-value explanatory question that makes the page more useful than a thin date page.

coverageMatrix.keyword: ما الفرق بين الصيف الفلكي والصيف المناخي
coverageMatrix.whyItMatters: This captures users who already noticed the two definitions and want them explained plainly.
This supports the science-first search phrasing.
This connects the summer-start query to the public-facing longest-day intent.

ربط العد التنازلي بالشرح العلمي بدل الاكتفاء بتاريخ جامد

تبسيط معنى الانقلاب الصيفي من غير لغة مدرسية جافة

unansweredQuestions: هل الصيف يبدأ 20 يونيو أم 21 يونيو؟
لماذا تقول بعض الصفحات إن الصيف يبدأ في 1 يونيو؟
هل يوم بداية الصيف هو أطول نهار في السنة؟
هل بداية الصيف تعني أشد الحر فوراً؟
ما الفرق بين الصيف الفلكي والصيف المناخي؟
differentiationIdeas: ابدأ الصفحة بجواب سريع ثم انتقل مباشرة إلى حل الالتباس بين الصيف الفلكي والمناخي.
اجعل زاوية الصفحة تعليمية وعملية معاً: متى يبدأ الصيف، ولماذا تختلف الصفحات، وماذا يعني ذلك فعلياً للحرارة والطقس.
استبدل الربط السطحي بين بداية الصيف وأشد الحر بشرح أبسط عن تأخر ذروة الحرارة.
استخدم لغة عربية مبسطة تشبه شرح معلم جيد، لا مقالة فلكية جافة.

factSources.label: NASA Science: Seeing Equinoxes and Solstices from Space

notes: The strongest opportunity is not just the date. It is resolving three recurring Arabic confusions in one page: 1 June versus 20 or 21 June, time-zone shifts between 20 and 21 June, and the assumption that the longest day must also be the hottest.
